reasoning: The Lean signature encodes the informal question faithfully. The `recolor` function is characterized by `recolor isred d ↔ ∃ p q > 0, p < q ∧ isred p = isred q ∧ q - p = d`, which exactly captures "d ∈ D" (there exist two positive same-colored points at distance d); `isred p = isred q` equals `isred p ↔ isred q` by propext, capturing "same color". The conclusion `∀ isred, ∃ k, ∀ p > 0, (recolor^[k] isred) p` matches "for every initial coloring, after finitely many iterations every positive real is red". Although `isred` is typed on all of ℝ rather than only positive reals (as the comment notes), this boost is harmless because `recolor` only inspects values at positive arguments, so values on non-positive reals never propagate and the final `∀ p > 0` restriction selects exactly the relevant conclusion. The iff with `putnam_2022_b3_solution` is the standard Putnam yes/no encoding.
